typical_monthly_usd: $349-$479 (Contact to Engage tier) for a single-location independent AR shop; some sources cite Contact $294 / Communicate $379 / Engage $469
setup_fee_usd: $199 onboarding & integration fee for Contact plan; sometimes waived via demo promo ($300 off first month + free onboarding)
min_ad_spend_usd: None — Steer is a CRM/marketing-automation platform, not an ad-buying agency. No PPC ad spend required or managed.
contract_length_months: Annual (most common) or month-to-month; annual cannot be terminated for convenience mid-term
cancellation_policy: 30 days written notice required to help@steer.io. Annual subscriptions: notice must be given 30 days before annual term expires or it auto-renews. Month-to-month: terminates 30 days after the next billing cycle following notice. No refunds for prepaid amounts. Auto-renews if notice not given.
asset_ownership: Steer is SaaS — customer data (contacts, vehicles, RO history) is owned by client but lives in Steer's CRM. No website product, so no website-ownership dispute. Phone numbers used for SMS may be Steer-provisioned (portability unclear). Review widgets and customer vehicle web pages are hosted on Steer infrastructure.
pricing_transparency: Tiered + published on steer.io/pricing and steercrm.com/pricing-plans (three named tiers: Contact, Communicate/Engage variations). More transparent than most peers (Kukui, Hibu, Turnkey are all quote-only).
known_pain_points: Not fully automated — some workflows require manual triggering despite 'automation' marketing
Message inbox UX: hard to bulk-clear messages, must click each individually
Reported case of review-request SMS silently failing for months without Steer noticing until client flagged it
30-day notice + annual auto-renewal creates same lock-in pattern as Kukui/Hibu
No refunds for prepaid annual amounts

Pricing-tier name confusion post-rebrand (Mechanic Advisor → Steer): old sources cite Contact/Engage, newer cite Contact/Communicate/Engage; actual quoted price varies $294-$479 across same plan names in 2024-2026 listings
Limited functionality outside CRM/communication: no website, no SEO, no PPC, no display, no creative production
notable_clients: Advance Professional (Advance Auto Parts) program partner
Pit Crew Marketing co-marketing partner shops
Autoshop Solutions partner shops (legacy MA partnership)
pros: Lower entry price than Kukui ($294-$349 vs $499 floor)
Deep SMS integrations with major shop management systems (Tekmetric, Mitchell1, NAPA TRACS, Shop-Ware, R.O. Writer)
No ad spend dependency — predictable monthly cost
Industry-credible (R+W coverage, Advance Pro partnership, long history under Mechanic Advisor brand since 2010s)
Month-to-month option available (most peers force annual)
AR-vertical built — terminology and workflows match a service writer's day
cons: Point solution, not a full marketing stack — does NOT build websites, run Google Ads, manage SEO, or do creative
Shop still needs a separate website vendor and ad agency on top of Steer
Annual contract still auto-renews and is non-cancelable mid-term
No collision (CR) workflow support — assumes mechanical service-writer flow
Reported reliability gap (SMS silently failing) erodes trust in 'set it and forget it' positioning
Onboarding fee on lowest tier ($199) is a soft friction point
koffman_relevance: Wrong tool to compare 1:1 with Hibu — Steer is a CRM/communications platform, not a marketing stack. It would NOT replace Hibu's website + Google Ads + display. It would replace a separate retention/CRM layer that Koffman currently does not appear to have. At $349-$479/mo, Steer could plug into a PSG-managed stack (PSG website + PSG ads + Steer for customer retention SMS/email/reviews) and would NOT compete with Turnkey Marketing either — Turnkey is fractional CMO + ad management, Steer is the automation layer below it. Best fit: complement, not replacement. If Koffman wants one vendor doing everything, Kukui or Turnkey are closer comps; Steer is the right pick only if Koffman keeps website + ads in-house (or with PSG) and just needs the retention automation stack on top. Note: Steer does NOT serve the collision side at all, so it's purely an AR-side tool for the mechanical book of business.
uncertain: current 2026 tier names (Contact / Communicate / Engage vs Contact / Engage) — sources conflict
exact differential features between tiers (only headline pricing is public)
whether onboarding fee applies to all tiers or only Contact
whether SMS phone numbers are portable on cancellation
min_ad_spend_usd N/A (no ads product), but uncertain whether any tier requires minimum SMS volume commitments
